title: Constant Functions Supported In Generates
body:
I have a piece of code where I noticed a difference in behavior between verilator and a major commercial simulator - the commercial simulator threw an error saying a function was not a valid constant function despite me being quite sure it was (the only inputs were constants and parameters). I dug into it and found out that the 2018 language standard in section 13.4.3 specifically prohibits declaring a constant function inside a generate block, which is what I had done because the only use of that function was also within that same generate block. Moving the function outside of the generate resolved the problem.

I believe that verilator should emit either an error or warning for constant functions declared within a generate block in order to better comply with the standard.

_Can you attach an example that shows the issue?  (Must be openly licensed, ideally in test_regress format.)_
I can generate an example if required, just let me know.

_What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?_
v4.200 I have not tried in the latest git master, but can if needed to confirm.

_Would you be willing to try to fix Verilator yourself with assistance?_
No, sorry. Willing to help with testcases and verification of any changes though!
